Title: Identification and characterization of a stachyose synthase gene controlling reduced stachyose content in soybean

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Seed oligosaccharide component affecting feed digestibility and feed efficiency; reduced stachyose is beneficial for soybean meal use in monogastric animals
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a galactosyl group from galactinol to raffinose to form stachyose
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Functional STS promotes stachyose accumulation and lowers raffinose; the sts mutant causes ~90% reduction of seed stachyose to about 0.5% and increases raffinose accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A 33-bp deletion in exon 4 of soybean STS (Glyma19g40550) was identified in PI 603176A, causing an 11-amino-acid deletion in the protein. 2. The sts deletion allele co-segregated with low stachyose and high raffinose in three segregating populations and fit a single Mendelian factor. 3. Correlation analyses and progeny validation supported STS as the gene responsible for converting raffinose to stachyose in soybean seeds. 4. qRT-PCR showed similar transcript abundance among mutant and wild-type lines, indicating loss of protein function rather than transcriptional disruption.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: natural variant identification; candidate gene sequencing; allele-specific marker development; genetic mapping/co-segregation in F2:3 and F4:5 populations; HPLC metabolite profiling; qRT-PCR expression analysis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in leaf at V1/V2 and R1 stages and in seed at R5 and R6; not tissue specific; similar transcript levels among parental lines
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in leaf and developing seed across vegetative and reproductive stages; slight increase in PI 603176A from V1 to R6
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control step for raffinose-to-stachyose conversion; single gene with major effect established in this study
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Multiple environments for stability testing; different growing environments in validation populations
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Low stachyose allele is associated with high raffinose, preserving total RFOs at a regular level; optimal raffinose/stachyose balance for feed is not fully determined
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A gene-based perfect marker for the sts deletion can be used for marker-assisted selection; the sts allele can be combined with RS2/RS3/RS4 mutant alleles to generate improved seed oligosaccharide composition
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mutant still retains ~0.5% stachyose, possibly due to partial residual activity or contribution from other related enzymes such as RS3/RS4; 33-bp deletion is in exon 4
